medications.indication_rationale: Do not initiate a benzodiazepine over telehealth; not the safe first step and unnecessary once danger signs are excluded.
Low-dose amitriptyline can help bruxism/central pain long-term but should be GP-initiated/titrated, not started acutely over chat.

medications.drug_name: simple analgesia (paracetamol ± short NSAID) and warm compresses
medications.indication_rationale: Simple analgesia within limits (already maxed OTC today — do not exceed) plus heat eases myofascial spasm while arranging review.
Not appropriate to initiate over chat for acute TMD.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Dentist for TMD/splint assessment and to exclude a dental cause.
TMJ/jaw physiotherapy for myofascial spasm once acute danger signs are excluded.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Warm moist compresses, jaw rest, soft/liquid diet
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Standard supportive measures that ease masseter spasm and maintain nutrition.
Limited evidence; not acute therapy and cannabinoids not to be initiated remotely.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for swelling under the chin/neck with drooling or breathing difficulty, sudden vision change, or the jaw becoming stuck open, and call 000/seek urgent care if they occur.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Safety-nets deep-space infection/airway, GCA and dislocation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take soft/blended/liquid meals and sip fluids (a straw may help) to maintain hydration/nutrition; use warm compresses and rest the jaw.

patient_education_points.point: Your jaw muscles have gone into a severe cramp from grinding, which is why the jaw is locked and the pain shoots to your temples — this is a temporomandibular (jaw-muscle) problem, made worse by your fibromyalgia.

Because we can't examine your mouth and jaw over a chat, and to be safe about a deep infection, you should be seen in person soon (dentist/GP) — but this is very unlikely to be anything dangerous given your normal breathing and no neck swelling.
Use warm compresses, rest the jaw, and stick to soft/liquid meals and sips of fluid; strong sedatives or nerve tablets shouldn't be started over a chat.

Call 000 / seek urgent care if you develop swelling under your chin or neck with drooling or trouble breathing, any sudden change in your vision, or your jaw becomes stuck open and you can't close it.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent in-person dental/GP review within 1–2 days; TMJ physiotherapy/dental splint for ongoing management
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Jaw/oral examination, hydration, analgesia plan, bruxism/splint management
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Neck/chin swelling with breathing difficulty, vision change, or a jaw stuck open — call 000/urgent care.

must_include_items: Screen deep-space infection/airway (neck/chin swelling, drooling, breathing), GCA (scalp tenderness/jaw claudication/vision) and dislocation (jaw stuck open)
Do NOT initiate benzodiazepines/tricyclics/cannabinoids over chat
Advise supportive care (heat, jaw rest, soft/liquid diet, simple analgesia) and hydration
acceptable_alternatives: GP-initiated low-dose amitriptyline later for bruxism
errors_of_commission: Recommending more OTC analgesia beyond limits
Reflexive ambulance for uncomplicated myofascial trismus
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) an airway/deep-space-infection + GCA + dislocation screen, (2) no remote benzodiazepine/tricyclic/cannabinoid initiation, and (3) supportive care/hydration with an in-person dental/GP review. Missing the airway/deep-space-infection screen is the critical failure.
